photos.title: Munetoshi Bloomery Iron Gyuto.jpg
photos.author: Rivenwyrm
photos.caption: Munetoshi Bloomery Iron Gyuto with Wa Handle. Blacksmith is Kouiti Turumaki, made from 500 year old french anchor from bloomery iron with (130WCrV5) steel core similar to blue 1. Special Thinned and Polished with Uchigumori and finished with Jizuya fingerstones, Spine and Choil are polished.

photos.caption: A 165mm nakiri , made from AUS-10 stainless steel (hammered damascus).

photos.title: 180mm Ashi Ginga Carbon Steel Santoku (2026)-104A8749.jpg
photos.author: Frank Schulenburg
photos.caption: An Ashi Ginga White #2 Carbon Steel Santoku, 180 mm, made by Ashi Hamono in Japan. The knife has a thin monosteel blade made from Shirogami #2, with a Western-style handle.

photos.title: Santoku-Kai-Shun-Nagare.jpg
photos.author: Simon A. Eugster
photos.caption: Santoku knife, made by Kai, Shun Nagare series.

photos.title: Masashi Kokuen Kiritsuke Petty 135mm (2026)-104A7479.jpg
photos.author: Frank Schulenburg
photos.caption: A 135 mm Japanese kiritsuke-style petty knife intended for small, precise cutting tasks such as trimming and slicing on a cutting board. It has an SLD semi-stainless steel core, a dark-finished blade, and a profile combining a relatively flat edge with a pointed tip for detail work. It is made by blacksmith Masashi Yamamoto in Sanjō , Niigata, Japan, a major center of Japanese knife production.

photos.title: Moritaka 8.25-Inch Aogami Super Carbon Steel Gyuto (2026)-104A7336.jpg
photos.author: Frank Schulenburg
photos.caption: A hand-forged Japanese chef’s knife (gyūtō) with an 8.25-inch blade made from Aogami Super carbon steel by Moritaka Hamono (盛高刃物). The blade features a dark kurouchi finish on the upper portion and a polished edge bevel along the cutting edge. The knife is fitted with a traditional Japanese wa-style wooden handle.

photos.title: Hitohira Togashi 165mm Deba Shirogami 2 (2026)-104A7987.jpg
photos.author: Frank Schulenburg
photos.caption: A 165mm Hitohira Togashi Deba, handmade in Sakai, with an iron-clad Shirogami #2 blade, forged by dentō kōgeishi master smith Kenji Togashi and ground by his son, Kenya.

photos.title: Deba Shouren Ginsan stainless steel Knife, Jikko Knives, Sakai (49070649923).jpg
photos.author: Stephan Ridgway from Brisbane, Australia
photos.caption: 實光刃物の出刃包丁

photos.title: Steelport 6-Inch Chef Knife (2026)-104A7331.jpg
photos.author: Frank Schulenburg
photos.caption: A 6-inch hand-forged chef knife made by Steelport Knife Co. in Portland, Oregon. The blade is crafted from American 52100 high-carbon steel, heat-treated for edge retention and sharpened to a fine, thin geometry. The knife features a full tang construction and is finished with contoured Oregon Big Leaf Maple burl handle scales secured with brass hardware.

photos.title: Chef Knives, German, Japanese, Chinese.jpg
photos.author: Olaf Simons
photos.caption: Three chef knives. A Japanese santoku (Global Yoshikin G-46), a German standard Kochmesser (Zwilling Profection) and a typical Chinese chef's knife
photos.displayTitle: German Japanese and Chinese chef knives

photos.title: Victorinox kitchen cutlery.jpg
photos.author: Francis Flinch
photos.caption: Top to bottom: Victorinox Fibrox 6.7703 pointed blade paring knife, polypropylene handle Victorinox Fibrox 5.0733 pointed blade wavy edge paring knife (single edge serrations), polypropylene handle Victorinox Fibrox 6.8633.21B bread knife with wavy edge (single edge serrations), polypropylene handle Victorinox Fibrox 5.2063.20 chef's knife, thermoplastic elastomer handle These are Swiss made stamped DIN 1.4110 or x50CrMov15 stainless steel knives, featuring cutting edges sharpened at 15 degree angles for V-shaped edges or 24 degrees for non-symmetric wavy edges and thermoplastic elastomers (TPE) polymer or polypropylene grips. They are dishwasher safe, but hand cleaning the blades and grips with soap and luke warm water is recommended.

photos.title: Kitchen knife made of stainless steel with sliced white cabbage.jpg
photos.author: Marc-Lautenbacher
photos.caption: A sharp kitchen knife, made of stainless steel with some just-cut white cabbage in evening light atmosphere on kitchen table. Preparation of a meal with white cabbage.
